```
 101: 
 102:     private fun selectBestRoot(document: Document): Element? {
 103:         val primarySelectors = listOf(
 104:             "[itemprop=articleBody]",
 105:             "[data-article-body]",
 106:             "[data-module*=article]",
 107:             ".article-body",
 108:             ".article__body",
 109:             ".article-content",
 110:             ".article__content",
 111:             ".story-body",
 112:             ".story-content",
 113:             ".entry-content",
 114:             ".post-content",
 115:             ".node__content",
 116:             ".wysiwyg",
 117:             "article .content",
 118:             "article .body",
 119:             "main article",
 120:             "article"
 121:         )
 122: 
 123:         primarySelectors.forEach { selector ->
 124:             document.select(selector)
 125:                 .firstOrNull { candidate -> isValidContentRoot(candidate) }
 126:                 ?.let { return it }
 127:         }
 128: 
 129:         val candidates = document.select("article, main, [role=main], section, div")
 130:             .distinct()
 131: 
 132:         return candidates.maxByOrNull { element ->
 133:             scoreContentRoot(element)
 134:         }?.takeIf(::isValidContentRoot)
 135:     }
 136: 
 137:     private fun extractReadableText(root: Element?): String {
 138:         if (root == null) return ""
 139: 
 140:         val workingRoot = root.clone()
 141:         workingRoot.select("*").toList().forEach { element ->
 142:             if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
 143:                 element.remove()
 144:             }
 145:         }
 146: 
 147:         val blocks = mutableListOf<String>()
 148:         val seen = linkedSetOf<String>()
 149: 
 150:         for (element in workingRoot.select("h2, h3, h4, p, li, blockquote, pre")) {
 151:             if (shouldSkipTextElement(element)) continue
 152: 
 153:             val text = normalizeText(element.text())
 154:             if (text.isBlank()) continue
 155: 
 156:             if (isTerminalSectionHeading(text, element)) {
 157:                 break
 158:             }
 159: 
 160:             if (isSkippableStandaloneText(text, element)) continue
 161: 
 162:             val minimumLength = if (element.tagName().startsWith("h")) 12 else 28
 163:             if (text.length < minimumLength) continue
 164:             if (seen.add(text)) {
 165:                 blocks += text
 166:             }
 167:         }
 168: 
 169:         if (blocks.isNotEmpty()) {
 170:             return blocks.joinToString(separator = "\n\n")
 171:         }
 172: 
 173:         return normalizeText(workingRoot.text())
 174:             .split(Regex("(?<=[.!?ã€‚ï¼ï¼Ÿ])\\s+"))
 175:             .map(::normalizeText)
 176:             .filter { it.length >= 30 }
 177:             .distinct()
 178:             .joinToString(separator = "\n\n")
 179:     }
 180: 
 181:     private fun resolveTitle(document: Document, normalizedUrl: String): String {
 182:         val metaTitle = document.selectFirst("meta[property=og:title]")?.attr("content")
 183:         val title = metaTitle?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 184:             ?: document.title().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 185:             ?: normalizedUrl
 186: 
 187:         return title.trim()
 188:     }
 189: 
 190:     private fun extractStructuredArticle(document: Document): StructuredArticle {
 191:         val articleNodes = mutableListOf<JSONObject>()
 192: 
 193:         document.select("script[type=application/ld+json]").forEach { script ->
 194:             val json = script.data().trim()
 195:             if (json.isBlank()) return@forEach
 196: 
 197:             runCatching {
 198:                 collectStructuredArticleNodes(json, articleNodes)
 199:             }
 200:         }
 201: 
 202:         val bestNode = articleNodes.maxByOrNull { node ->
 203:             node.optString("articleBody").length * 3 +
 204:                 node.optString("description").length +
 205:                 node.optString("headline").length
 206:         }
 207: 
 208:         return StructuredArticle(
 209:             title = bestNode?.optString("headline")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
 210:             articleBody = bestNode?.optString("articleBody")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 211:                 ?: bestNode?.optString("description")?.takeIf { it.length >= 180 },
 212:             languageCode = bestNode?.optString("inLanguage")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 213:         )
 214:     }
 215: 
 216:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(json: String, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 217:         val trimmed = json.trim()
 218:         when {
 219:             trimmed.startsWith("[") -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(JSONArray(trimmed), sink)
 220:             trimmed.startsWith("{") -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(JSONObject(trimmed), sink)
 221:         }
 222:     }
 223: 
 224:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(array: JSONArray, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 225:         for (index in 0 until array.length()) {
 226:             when (val item = array.opt(index)) {
 227:                 is JSONObject -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(item, sink)
 228:                 is JSONArray -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(item, sink)
 229:             }
 230:         }
 231:     }
 232: 
 233:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(node: JSONObject, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 234:         if (node.isLikelyArticleNode()) {
 235:             sink += node
 236:         }
 237: 
 238:         node.optJSONArray("@graph")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 239:         node.optJSONObject("mainEntity")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 240:         node.optJSONObject("mainEntityOfPage")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 241:     }
 242: 
 243:     private fun JSONObject.isLikelyArticleNode(): Boolean {
 244:         val typeValue = opt("@type")
 245:         val types = when (typeValue) {
 246:             is JSONArray -> buildList {
 247:                 for (index in 0 until typeValue.length()) {
 248:                     add(typeValue.optString(index))
 249:                 }
 250:             }
 251: 
 252:             else -> listOf(optString("@type"))
 253:         }.map { it.lowercase() }
 254: 
 255:         return types.any { type ->
 256:             type.contains("article") || type.contains("newsarticle") || type.contains("report")
 257:         }
 258:     }
 259: 
 260:     private fun isValidContentRoot(element: Element): Boolean {
 261:         if (element == null) return false
 262:         if (shouldPruneElement(element)) return false
 263:         if (element.text().length < 180) return false
 264:         return scoreContentRoot(element) >= 280
 265:     }
 266: 
 267:     private fun scoreContentRoot(element: Element): Int {
 268:         val paragraphCount = element.select("p").size
 269:         val paragraphTextLength = element.select("p").sumOf { it.text().length }
 270:         val articleHint = if (hasArticleHint(element)) 250 else 0
 271:         val bodyHint = if (hasBodyHint(element)) 180 else 0
 272:         val junkPenalty = if (hasJunkHint(element)) 500 else 0
 273: 
 274:         return paragraphTextLength + (paragraphCount * 120) + articleHint + bodyHint - junkPenalty
 275:     }
 276: 
 277:     private fun shouldPruneElement(element: Element): Boolean {
 278:         val ownText = normalizeText(element.ownText())
 279:         val combinedHints = buildString {
 280:             append(element.tagName())
 281:             append(' ')
 282:             append(element.id())
 283:             append(' ')
 284:             append(element.className())
 285:             append(' ')
 286:             append(element.attr("role"))
 287:             append(' ')
 288:             append(element.attr("aria-label"))
 289:             append(' ')
 290:             append(element.attr("data-testid"))
 291:             append(' ')
 292:             append(element.attr("data-module"))
 293:         }.lowercase()
 294: 
 295:         if (combinedHints.contains("article-body") || combinedHints.contains("article__body")) {
 296:             return false
 297:         }
 298: 
 299:         if (combinedHints.anyContains(PRUNE_KEYWORDS)) {
 300:             return true
 301:         }
 302: 
 303:         if (combinedHints.contains("ad") && !hasArticleHint(element)) {
 304:             return true
 305:         }
 306: 
 307:         return isStandaloneJunkHeading(ownText)
 308:     }
 309: 
 310:     private fun shouldSkipTextElement(element: Element): Boolean {
 311:         val text = normalizeText(element.text())
 312:         if (text.isBlank()) return true
 313:         if (element.parents().any(::shouldPruneElement)) return true
 314:         if (text.anyContains(SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS)) return true
 315:         return false
 316:     }
 317: 
 318:     private fun isSkippableStandaloneText(text: String, element: Element): Boolean {
 319:         if (text.anyContains(SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS)) return true
 320:         if (element.tagName() == "li" && text.length < 45) return true
 321:         return false
 322:     }
 323: 
 324:     private fun isTerminalSectionHeading(text: String, element: Element): Boolean {
 325:         if (!element.tagName().startsWith("h")) return false
 326: 
 327:         val normalized = text.lowercase()
 328:         return TERMINAL_SECTION_HEADINGS.any { marker ->
 329:             normalized == marker || normalized.startsWith("$marker:")
 330:         }
 331:     }
 332: 
 333:     private fun isStandaloneJunkHeading(text: String): Boolean {
 334:         if (text.isBlank()) return false
 335:         val normalized = text.lowercase()
 336:         return STANDALONE_JUNK_HEADINGS.any { marker ->
 337:             normalized == marker || normalized.startsWith("$marker:")
 338:         }
 339:     }
 340: 
 341:     private fun looksPaywalled(document: Document, extractedText: String): Boolean {
 342:         val pageText = normalizeText(document.text())
 343:         if (extractedText.length >= 220) return false
 344: 
 345:         return pageText.anyContains(
 346:             listOf(
 347:                 "unlocking article",
 348:                 "subscribe to continue",
 349:                 "subscriber only",
 350:                 "sign in to read",
 351:                 "login to continue",
 352:                 "members only"
 353:             )
 354:         )
 355:     }
 356: 
 357:     private fun hasArticleHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 358:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()} ${element.attr("itemprop")}".lowercase()
 359:         return hints.anyContains(listOf("article", "story", "entry", "post", "content"))
 360:     }
 361: 
 362:     private fun hasBodyHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 363:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()} ${element.attr("itemprop")}".lowercase()
 364:         return hints.anyContains(listOf("body", "main", "text", "copy", "content"))
 365:     }
 366: 
 367:     private fun hasJunkHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 368:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()}".lowercase()
 369:         return hints.anyContains(PRUNE_KEYWORDS)
 370:     }
 371: 
 372:     private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
 373:         return text
 374:             .replace('\u00A0', ' ')
 375:             .replace(Regex("[\\t\\x0B\\f\\r ]+"), " ")
 376:             .replace(Regex("\\n{3,}"), "\n\n")
 377:             .lines()
 378:             .map { it.trim() }
 379:             .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
 380:             .joinToString("\n")
 381:             .trim()
 382:     }
 383: 
 384:     private fun String.anyContains(values: List<String>): Boolean {
```

```
   1: package com.scribespeak.app.data.web

   3: import com.scribespeak.app.domain.extract.WebArticleExtractor
   4: import com.scribespeak.app.domain.extract.WebArticleResult

   8: import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
   9: import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext

  13: import org.json.JSONObject

  17: 
  18: @Singleton
  19: class JsoupWebArticleExtractor @Inject constructor(
  20:     private val okHttpClient: OkHttpClient
  21: ) : WebArticleExtractor {
  22:     override suspend fun extract(url: String): WebArticleResult {
  23:         return withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
  24:             val normalizedUrl = normalizeUrl(url)
  25:             val request = Request.Builder()
  26:                 .url(normalizedUrl)
  27:                 .header(
  28:                     "User-Agent",
  29:                     "Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 14) AppleWebKit/537.36 " +
  30:                         "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0 Mobile Safari/537.36"
  31:                 )
  32:                 .build()
  33: 
  34:             val response = okHttpClient.newCall(request).execute()
  35:             if (!response.isSuccessful) {
  36:                 response.close()
  37:                 throw IllegalStateException("Request failed with HTTP ${response.code}")
  38:             }
  39: 
  40:             val html = response.body?.string().orEmpty()
  41:             response.close()
  42: 
  43:             if (html.isBlank()) {
  44:                 throw IllegalStateException("The page returned empty content.")
  45:             }
  46: 
  47:             parseDocument(
  48:                 document = Jsoup.parse(html, normalizedUrl),
  49:                 normalizedUrl = normalizedUrl
  50:             )
  51:         }
  52:     }
  53: 
  54:     internal fun parseDocument(document: Document, normalizedUrl: String): WebArticleResult {
  55:         val structuredArticle = extractStructuredArticle(document)
  56:         sanitize(document)
  57: 
  58:         val articleRoot = selectBestRoot(document)
  59:         val extractedText = structuredArticle.articleBody
  60:             ?.let(::normalizeText)
  61:             ?.takeIf { it.length >= 180 }
  62:             ?: extractReadableText(articleRoot ?: document.body())
  63: 
  64:         if (extractedText.length < 120) {
  65:             throw IllegalStateException(
  66:                 if (looksPaywalled(document, extractedText)) {
  67:                     "That page appears to hide the full article behind a paywall or app-only gate."
  68:                 } else {
  69:                     "Couldn't find enough readable article text on that page."
  70:                 }
  71:             )
  72:         }
  73: 
  74:         val title = structuredArticle.title
  75:             ?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
  76:             ?: resolveTitle(document, normalizedUrl)
  77:         val languageCode = structuredArticle.languageCode
  78:             ?.substringBefore('-')
  79:             ?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
  80:             ?: document.selectFirst("html")?.attr("lang")
  81:                 ?.substringBefore('-')
  82:                 ?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
  83: 
  84:         return WebArticleResult(
  85:             normalizedUrl = normalizedUrl,
  86:             title = title,
  87:             extractedText = extractedText,
  88:             languageCode = languageCode
  89:         )
  90:     }
  91: 
  92:     private fun sanitize(document: Document) {
  93:         document.select(
  94:             "script:not([type=application/ld+json]), style, nav, footer, header, " +
  95:                 "noscript, form, iframe, svg, button, input, picture, source, video, audio, canvas"
  96:         ).remove()
  97: 
  98:         document.select("*").toList().forEach { element ->
  99:             if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
 100:                 element.remove()
 101:             }
 102:         }
 103:     }
 104: 
 105:     private fun selectBestRoot(document: Document): Element? {
 106:         val primarySelectors = listOf(
 107:             "[itemprop=articleBody]",
 108:             "[data-article-body]",
 109:             "[data-module*=article]",
 110:             ".article-body",
 111:             ".article__body",
 112:             ".article-content",
 113:             ".article__content",
 114:             ".story-body",
 115:             ".story-content",
 116:             ".entry-content",
 117:             ".post-content",
 118:             ".node__content",
 119:             ".wysiwyg",
 120:             "article .content",
 121:             "article .body",
 122:             "main article",
 123:             "article"
 124:         )
 125: 
 126:         primarySelectors.forEach { selector ->
 127:             document.select(selector)
 128:                 .firstOrNull { candidate -> isValidContentRoot(candidate) }
 129:                 ?.let { return it }
 130:         }
 131: 
 132:         val candidates = document.select("article, main, [role=main], section, div")
 133:             .distinct()
 134: 
 135:         return candidates.maxByOrNull(::scoreContentRoot)
 136:             ?.takeIf(::isValidContentRoot)
 137:     }
 138: 
 139:     private fun extractReadableText(root: Element?): String {
 140:         if (root == null) return ""
 141: 
 142:         val workingRoot = root.clone()
 143:         workingRoot.select("*").toList().forEach { element ->
 144:             if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
 145:                 element.remove()
 146:             }
 147:         }
 148: 
 149:         val blocks = mutableListOf<String>()
 150:         val seen = linkedSetOf<String>()
 151: 
 152:         for (element in workingRoot.select("h2, h3, h4, p, li, blockquote, pre")) {
 153:             if (shouldSkipTextElement(element)) continue
 154: 
 155:             val text = normalizeText(element.text())
 156:             if (text.isBlank()) continue
 157: 
 158:             if (isTerminalSectionHeading(text, element)) {
 159:                 break
 160:             }
 161: 
 162:             if (isSkippableStandaloneText(text, element)) continue
 163: 
 164:             val minimumLength = if (element.tagName().startsWith("h")) 12 else 28
 165:             if (text.length < minimumLength) continue
 166: 
 167:             if (seen.add(text)) {
 168:                 blocks += text
 169:             }
 170:         }
 171: 
 172:         if (blocks.isNotEmpty()) {
 173:             return blocks.joinToString(separator = "\n\n")
 174:         }
 175: 
 176:         return normalizeText(workingRoot.text())
 177:             .split(Regex("(?<=[.!?\\u3002\\uFF01\\uFF1F])\\s+"))
 178:             .map(::normalizeText)
 179:             .filter { it.length >= 30 }
 180:             .distinct()
 181:             .joinToString(separator = "\n\n")
 182:     }
 183: 
 184:     private fun resolveTitle(document: Document, normalizedUrl: String): String {
 185:         val metaTitle = document.selectFirst("meta[property=og:title]")?.attr("content")
 186:         val title = metaTitle?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 187:             ?: document.title().takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 188:             ?: normalizedUrl
 189: 
 190:         return title.trim()
 191:     }
 192: 
 193:     private fun extractStructuredArticle(document: Document): StructuredArticle {
 194:         val articleNodes = mutableListOf<JSONObject>()
 195: 
 196:         document.select("script[type=application/ld+json]").forEach { script ->
 197:             val json = script.data().trim()
 198:             if (json.isBlank()) return@forEach
 199: 
 200:             runCatching {
 201:                 collectStructuredArticleNodes(json, articleNodes)
 202:             }
 203:         }
 204: 
 205:         val bestNode = articleNodes.maxByOrNull { node ->
 206:             node.optString("articleBody").length * 3 +
 207:                 node.optString("description").length +
 208:                 node.optString("headline").length
 209:         }
 210: 
 211:         return StructuredArticle(
 212:             title = bestNode?.optString("headline")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
 213:             articleBody = bestNode?.optString("articleBody")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 214:                 ?: bestNode?.optString("description")?.takeIf { it.length >= 180 },
 215:             languageCode = bestNode?.optString("inLanguage")?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
 216:         )
 217:     }
 218: 
 219:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(json: String, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 220:         val trimmed = json.trim()
 221:         when {
 222:             trimmed.startsWith("[") -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(JSONArray(trimmed), sink)
 223:             trimmed.startsWith("{") -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(JSONObject(trimmed), sink)
 224:         }
 225:     }
 226: 
 227:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(array: JSONArray, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 228:         for (index in 0 until array.length()) {
 229:             when (val item = array.opt(index)) {
 230:                 is JSONObject -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(item, sink)
 231:                 is JSONArray -> collectStructuredArticleNodes(item, sink)
 232:             }
 233:         }
 234:     }
 235: 
 236:     private fun collectStructuredArticleNodes(node: JSONObject, sink: MutableList<JSONObject>) {
 237:         if (node.isLikelyArticleNode()) {
 238:             sink += node
 239:         }
 240: 
 241:         node.optJSONArray("@graph")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 242:         node.optJSONObject("mainEntity")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 243:         node.optJSONObject("mainEntityOfPage")?.let { collectStructuredArticleNodes(it, sink) }
 244:     }
 245: 
 246:     private fun JSONObject.isLikelyArticleNode(): Boolean {
 247:         val typeValue = opt("@type")
 248:         val types = when (typeValue) {
 249:             is JSONArray -> buildList {
 250:                 for (index in 0 until typeValue.length()) {
 251:                     add(typeValue.optString(index))
 252:                 }
 253:             }
 254: 
 255:             else -> listOf(optString("@type"))
 256:         }.map { it.lowercase() }
 257: 
 258:         return types.any { type ->
 259:             type.contains("article") || type.contains("newsarticle") || type.contains("report")
 260:         }
 261:     }
 262: 
 263:     private fun isValidContentRoot(element: Element): Boolean {
 264:         if (shouldPruneElement(element)) return false
 265:         if (element.text().length < 180) return false
 266:         return scoreContentRoot(element) >= 280
 267:     }
 268: 
 269:     private fun scoreContentRoot(element: Element): Int {
 270:         val paragraphCount = element.select("p").size
 271:         val paragraphTextLength = element.select("p").sumOf { it.text().length }
 272:         val articleHint = if (hasArticleHint(element)) 250 else 0
 273:         val bodyHint = if (hasBodyHint(element)) 180 else 0
 274:         val junkPenalty = if (hasJunkHint(element)) 500 else 0
 275: 
 276:         return paragraphTextLength + (paragraphCount * 120) + articleHint + bodyHint - junkPenalty
 277:     }
 278: 
 279:     private fun shouldPruneElement(element: Element): Boolean {
 280:         val ownText = normalizeText(element.ownText())
 281:         val combinedHints = buildString {
 282:             append(element.tagName())
 283:             append(' ')
 284:             append(element.id())
 285:             append(' ')
 286:             append(element.className())
 287:             append(' ')
 288:             append(element.attr("role"))
 289:             append(' ')
 290:             append(element.attr("aria-label"))
 291:             append(' ')
 292:             append(element.attr("data-testid"))
 293:             append(' ')
 294:             append(element.attr("data-module"))
 295:         }.lowercase()
 296: 
 297:         if (combinedHints.contains("article-body") || combinedHints.contains("article__body")) {
 298:             return false
 299:         }
 300: 
 301:         if (combinedHints.anyContains(PRUNE_KEYWORDS)) {
 302:             return true
 303:         }
 304: 
 305:         if (
 306:             element.tagName() !in setOf("article", "main", "body") &&
 307:             element.select("h1, h2, h3, h4, h5").any { heading ->
 308:                 isStandaloneJunkHeading(normalizeText(heading.text()))
 309:             }
 310:         ) {
 311:             return true
 312:         }
 313: 
 314:         if (hasAdLikeHint(combinedHints) && !hasArticleHint(element)) {
 315:             return true
 316:         }
 317: 
 318:         return isStandaloneJunkHeading(ownText)
 319:     }
 320: 
 321:     private fun shouldSkipTextElement(element: Element): Boolean {
 322:         val text = normalizeText(element.text())
 323:         if (text.isBlank()) return true
 324:         if (element.parents().any(::shouldPruneElement)) return true
 325:         if (text.anyContains(SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS)) return true
 326:         return false
 327:     }
 328: 
 329:     private fun isSkippableStandaloneText(text: String, element: Element): Boolean {
 330:         if (text.anyContains(SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS)) return true
 331:         if (element.tagName() == "li" && text.length < 45) return true
 332:         return false
 333:     }
 334: 
 335:     private fun isTerminalSectionHeading(text: String, element: Element): Boolean {
 336:         if (!element.tagName().startsWith("h")) return false
 337: 
 338:         val normalized = text.lowercase()
 339:         return TERMINAL_SECTION_HEADINGS.any { marker ->
 340:             normalized == marker || normalized.startsWith("$marker:")
 341:         }
 342:     }
 343: 
 344:     private fun isStandaloneJunkHeading(text: String): Boolean {
 345:         if (text.isBlank()) return false
 346:         val normalized = text.lowercase()
 347:         return STANDALONE_JUNK_HEADINGS.any { marker ->
 348:             normalized == marker || normalized.startsWith("$marker:")
 349:         }
 350:     }
 351: 
 352:     private fun looksPaywalled(document: Document, extractedText: String): Boolean {
 353:         val pageText = normalizeText(document.text())
 354:         if (extractedText.length >= 220) return false
 355: 
 356:         return pageText.anyContains(
 357:             listOf(
 358:                 "unlocking article",
 359:                 "subscribe to continue",
 360:                 "subscriber only",
 361:                 "sign in to read",
 362:                 "login to continue",
 363:                 "members only"
 364:             )
 365:         )
 366:     }
 367: 
 368:     private fun hasArticleHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 369:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()} ${element.attr("itemprop")}".lowercase()
 370:         return hints.anyContains(listOf("article", "story", "entry", "post", "content"))
 371:     }
 372: 
 373:     private fun hasBodyHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 374:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()} ${element.attr("itemprop")}".lowercase()
 375:         return hints.anyContains(listOf("body", "main", "text", "copy", "content"))
 376:     }
 377: 
 378:     private fun hasJunkHint(element: Element): Boolean {
 379:         val hints = "${element.id()} ${element.className()}".lowercase()
 380:         return hints.anyContains(PRUNE_KEYWORDS)
 381:     }
 382: 
 383:     private fun hasAdLikeHint(text: String): Boolean {
 384:         return Regex("(^|[^a-z])(ad|ads|advert|adslot)([^a-z]|$)").containsMatchIn(text)
 385:     }
 386: 
 387:     private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
 388:         return text
 389:             .replace('\u00A0', ' ')
 390:             .replace(Regex("[\\t\\x0B\\f\\r ]+"), " ")
 391:             .replace(Regex("\\n{3,}"), "\n\n")
 392:             .lines()
 393:             .map { it.trim() }
 394:             .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
 395:             .joinToString("\n")
 396:             .trim()
 397:     }
 398: 
 399:     private fun String.anyContains(values: List<String>): Boolean {
 400:         return values.any { value -> contains(value, ignoreCase = true) }
 401:     }
 402: 
 403:     private data class StructuredArticle(
 404:         val title: String? = null,
 405:         val articleBody: String? = null,
 406:         val languageCode: String? = null
 407:     )
 408: 
 409:     private companion object {
 410:         val PRUNE_KEYWORDS = listOf(
 411:             "recommend",
 412:             "related",
 413:             "most-read",
 414:             "mostread",
 415:             "most-commented",
 416:             "popular",
 417:             "trending",
 418:             "share",
 419:             "social",
 420:             "newsletter",
 421:             "subscribe",
 422:             "comment",
 423:             "cookie",
 424:             "consent",
 425:             "breadcrumb",
 426:             "promo",
 427:             "advert",
 428:             "ad-container",
 429:             "adslot",
 430:             "taboola",
 431:             "outbrain",
 432:             "next-post",
 433:             "read-more",
 434:             "more-like-this",
 435:             "tag-list",
 436:             "topics",
 437:             "paywall",
 438:             "locked",
 439:             "unlocking",
 440:             "audio-broadcast",
 441:             "call-to-action"
 442:         )
 443: 
 444:         val SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS = listOf(
 445:             "recommended stories",
 446:             "related reports",
 447:             "read more like this",
 448:             "view comments",
 449:             "unlocking article",
 450:             "help us get the word out",
 451:             "audio broadcast",
 452:             "call to action",
 453:             "please join the",
 454:             "advertisement",
 455:             "ads"
 456:         )
 457: 
 458:         val TERMINAL_SECTION_HEADINGS = listOf(
 459:             "tags",

 461:             "audio broadcast",
 462:             "call to action",
 463:             "related reports",
```

```
Exit code: 0

Output:
   1: package com.scribespeak.app.data.web
   2: 
   3: import okhttp3.OkHttpClient
   4: import org.jsoup.Jsoup
   5: import org.junit.Assert.assertEquals
   6: import org.junit.Assert.assertFalse
   7: import org.junit.Assert.assertTrue
   8: import org.junit.Test
   9: 
  10: class JsoupWebArticleExtractorTest {
  11:     private val extractor = JsoupWebArticleExtractor(OkHttpClient())
  12: 
  13:     @Test
  14:     fun `structured article body is preferred over noisy page chrome`() {
  15:         val html = """
  16:             <html lang="en">
  17:             <head>
  18:                 <title>Fallback title</title>
  19:                 <script type="application/ld+json">
  20:                 {
  21:                   "@context": "https://schema.org",
  22:                   "@type": "NewsArticle",
  23:                   "headline": "Structured title",
  24:                   "inLanguage": "en-US",
  25:                   "articleBody": "First real paragraph with enough words to be meaningful and clearly part of the article.\n\nSecond real paragraph continues the story without any recommendation widgets or tags attached."
  26:                 }
  27:                 </script>
  28:             </head>
  29:             <body>
  30:                 <div class="recommended">Recommended Stories</div>
  31:                 <article>
  32:                     <p>Noise that should not win because structured data is better.</p>
  33:                 </article>
  34:             </body>
  35:             </html>
  36:         """.trimIndent()
  37: 
  38:         val result = extractor.parseDocument(Jsoup.parse(html, "https://example.com/story"), "https://example.com/story")
  39: 
  40:         assertEquals("Structured title", result.title)
  41:         assertEquals("en", result.languageCode)
  42:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.startsWith("First real paragraph"))
  43:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Recommended Stories"))
  44:     }
  45: 
  46:     @Test
  47:     fun `recommended stories and ads are removed from article body`() {
  48:         val html = """
  49:             <html lang="en">
  50:             <body>
  51:                 <article class="article-body">
  52:                     <h1>Headline</h1>
  53:                     <p>This opening paragraph contains enough text to count as the real beginning of the article and should be preserved fully.</p>
  54:                     <div class="recommended">
  55:                         <h2>Recommended Stories</h2>
  56:                         <p>This should never appear in the extracted output because it is not part of the main article.</p>
  57:                     </div>
  58:                     <p>This follow-up paragraph continues the reporting and should still appear after the recommendation block is stripped out.</p>
  59:                     <div class="advertisement">Advertisement</div>
  60:                     <p>This closing paragraph is also real article text and should remain available to the reader.</p>
  61:                 </article>
  62:             </body>
  63:             </html>
  64:         """.trimIndent()
  65: 
  66:         val result = extractor.parseDocument(Jsoup.parse(html, "https://example.com/article"), "https://example.com/article")
  67: 
  68:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.contains("opening paragraph"))
  69:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.contains("follow-up paragraph"))
  70:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.contains("closing paragraph"))
  71:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Recommended Stories"))
  72:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Advertisement"))
  73:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("never appear"))
  74:     }
  75: 
  76:     @Test
  77:     fun `tags and call to action sections are excluded`() {
  78:         val html = """
  79:             <html lang="en">
  80:             <body>
  81:                 <article class="post-content">
  82:                     <h1>Ministry leader urges caution as Christians turn to AI for guidance</h1>
  83:                     <p>USA (MNN) â€” This first paragraph is part of the story and contains enough substance to represent the article body properly for extraction.</p>
  84:                     <p>The second paragraph adds more reporting detail, context, and quoted material so the test resembles a realistic article structure.</p>
  85:                     <h4>Tags:</h4>
  86:                     <p>AI artificial intelligence barna research</p>
  87:                     <h3>Help us get the word out:</h3>
  88:                     <p>Share Tweet Share</p>
  89:                     <h3>Audio Broadcast</h3>
  90:                     <p>Download 1min 2min 4-5min</p>
  91:                 </article>
  92:             </body>
  93:             </html>
  94:         """.trimIndent()
  95: 
  96:         val result = extractor.parseDocument(Jsoup.parse(html, "https://example.com/mnn"), "https://example.com/mnn")
  97: 
  98:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.contains("first paragraph"))
  99:         assertTrue(result.extractedText.contains("second paragraph"))
 100:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Tags:"))
 101:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Help us get the word out"))
 102:         assertFalse(result.extractedText.contains("Audio Broadcast"))
 103:     }
 104: 
 105:     @Test
 106:     fun `paywall scaffolding returns a clearer error`() {
 107:         val html = """
 108:             <html lang="en">
 109:             <body>
 110:                 <main>
 111:                     <article>
 112:                         <h1>Locked story</h1>
 113:                         <p>This brief summary teases the story but is not enough to represent a full article for the reader.</p>
 114:                         <div>Unlocking Article</div>
 115:                         <div>Please subscribe to continue reading.</div>
 116:                     </article>
 117:                 </main>
 118:             </body>
 119:             </html>
 120:         """.trimIndent()
 121: 
 122:         val error = runCatching {
 123:             extractor.parseDocument(Jsoup.parse(html, "https://example.com/locked"), "https://example.com/locked")
 124:         }.exceptionOrNull()
 125: 
 126:         assertEquals(
 127:             "That page appears to hide the full article behind a paywall or app-only gate.",
 128:             error?.message
 129:         )
 130:     }
 131: }

```

## [#165] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:30:48`

I tightened the web extractor in [JsoupWebArticleExtractor.kt](C:/Users/Home/Desktop/Vibe%20Coding%20FYP/FYP_Vibe_Implementation/Codex/TTS_Application/app/src/main/java/com/scribespeak/app/data/web/JsoupWebArticleExtractor.kt:18) so it now does three things much more aggressively:

- prefers structured `NewsArticle` / `articleBody` data when a site exposes it
- picks a narrower article root using article-body/content selectors before falling back to generic scoring
- strips recommendation blocks, related stories, tags, share sections, ad containers, CTA sections, and paywall scaffolding before building the extracted text

I also added regression tests in [JsoupWebArticleExtractorTest.kt](C:/Users/Home/Desktop/Vibe%20Coding%20FYP/FYP_Vibe_Implementation/Codex/TTS_Application/app/src/test/java/com/scribespeak/app/data/web/JsoupWebArticleExtractorTest.kt:10) for:
- structured article-body extraction
- mid-article “Recommended Stories” removal
- trailing tags / CTA / audio sections removal
- clearer paywall failure behavior

I used your sample URLs and inspected the page structure patterns from:
- Al Jazeera: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/15/israel-approves-proposal-to-register-west-bank-lands-as-state-property
- Mission Network News: https://www.mnnonline.org/news/ministry-leader-urges-caution-as-christians-turn-to-ai-for-guidance/
- Malaysiakini: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/776371

One honest limitation: I couldn’t run a full local Gradle test/compile here because the wrapper download in this environment still fails on SSL certificate validation. Please build and try those URLs again in Android Studio. If any one of them still brings in junk text, send me the bad extracted output and I’ll tune the pruning rules against that exact failure.

I can run local file reads, edits, and code review here, but I can’t reliably run your Gradle build in this environment right now because the Gradle wrapper tries to download `gradle-8.11.1-bin.zip` and hits an SSL certificate validation failure. There are a few alternatives:

1. If Android Studio on your machine already syncs/builds, that’s the best verifier. You send me the exact compile/runtime errors and I fix them.

## [#172] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:34:02`

Yes, partly.

What I confirmed here:
- `java` is installed and runnable
- `jsoup` and `okhttp` jars already exist in your local Gradle cache
- `kotlinc` is not installed in this environment

So the practical split is:

- I can run a small standalone Java harness for HTML parsing/extraction logic, as long as it only uses jars that are already cached locally.
- I cannot directly execute the exact Kotlin app extractor source without either:
  - a successful Gradle/Kotlin build, or
  - a Kotlin compiler available separately.

So for text extraction specifically, there are two workable alternatives to Gradle:
1. Standalone Java probe
   - good for testing parsing on sample HTML or URLs
   - doesn’t need Android Studio or the Gradle wrapper
2. Fixture-driven parsing
   - feed saved HTML into a small parser harness
   - very good for repeatable extraction debugging

What I still can’t do with that approach:
- run the full Android app flow
- verify Hilt/Compose/Room integration
- download anything new if a dependency is missing

If you want, I can build a tiny standalone Java extractor runner in this repo next, so we can test article extraction outside Gradle and outside the Android app.

## [#187] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:47:21`

I added a standalone Java probe plus a one-command PowerShell wrapper:

- [tools/WebExtractorProbe.java](C:/Users/Home/Desktop/Vibe%20Coding%20FYP/FYP_Vibe_Implementation/Codex/TTS_Application/tools/WebExtractorProbe.java:1)
- [tools/run-extractor-probe.ps1](C:/Users/Home/Desktop/Vibe%20Coding%20FYP/FYP_Vibe_Implementation/Codex/TTS_Application/tools/run-extractor-probe.ps1:1)

You can run it like this from the project root:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\run-extractor-probe.ps1 -Url "https://example.com/article" -Insecure
```

`-Insecure` is there because this environment is hitting SSL certificate-chain issues on some sites.

I did run it here. The good news is: yes, we can execute a Java-based extractor outside Gradle now. The less good news is that the live sites I tried here returned effectively empty usable content through this path, which means this environment is still getting blocked by site/network/SSL behavior before the extractor logic really gets a fair shot.

So this tool is now ready for us to use, but for the next step I’d recommend one of these:
- run that command on one of your URLs and paste the output here
- or tell me to add an `HTML file` mode so we can save a raw page once and debug extraction deterministically from local HTML without live site interference

## [#189] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:49:26`

I can run the probe itself, and I did.

What I cannot reliably do here is get trustworthy live article HTML from those news sites in this environment. The blockers are environmental, not code-only:
- SSL trust-chain problems
- some sites likely serving restricted/empty/different content to this environment
- no Android app runtime here for a true end-to-end import test

So the honest answer is:
- I can run the standalone Java tool
- I cannot fully trust the live extraction results from this environment alone

That’s why the most reliable path is either:
- you run the probe locally on your machine and send me the output, or
- the app runs locally and you paste the bad extracted text, and I tune against that exact failure

If you want, I can make the probe even more useful by adding:
1. `--save-html` to dump the fetched page locally
2. `--input-file` to test extraction from saved HTML
3. debug output showing which selector/root was chosen

That would let us debug extraction very precisely without depending on flaky live fetch behavior every time.

# Context from my IDE setup:

## My request for Codex:
what is SSL trust chain? I thought you encountered problems with android studio only?

## [#191] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:50:05`

SSL trust chain is a certificate validation check Java does before it accepts an `https://` website connection.

In simple terms:
- a site presents a security certificate
- Java tries to verify that certificate through a chain of trusted certificate authorities
- if that chain can’t be validated, Java refuses the connection with errors like `PKIX path building failed`

So this is not just an Android Studio problem.

It affected multiple things here:
- `Gradle wrapper` download
- standalone Java HTTP requests from the probe
- likely any Java-based network call in this environment to certain HTTPS sites

Android Studio on your machine may still work fine because:
- your local machine can have a different trust store
- Android Studio may already have downloaded what it needs
- your device/emulator runtime is separate from this sandboxed environment

So there are two environments:
- your machine / Android Studio: often works
- this Codex execution environment: has SSL certificate trust issues for some HTTPS connections

That’s why I can edit code confidently, but live network verification from here is less reliable than testing on your side.

## [#193] Assistant
- Timestamp: `2026-05-31 08:51:02`

If you want me to be able to test extraction standalone reliably, there are three practical paths.

Best short-term option:
- Let me add `--input-file` mode to the probe.
- You save the raw HTML of a problem article once, then I can run extraction against that local file with no SSL/network issues.
- This is the most reliable way to debug selectors and junk-removal.

Fastest workaround:
- Use the probe with `-Insecure`.
- That skips certificate validation for debugging only.
- It’s okay for local testing, but not something we should use in the app itself.

Proper long-term fix:
- Fix Java’s trust store on your machine so Java can verify HTTPS normally.
- This usually means one of these:
  - use a JDK that already trusts your network’s certificates
  - import your organization/antivirus/proxy root certificate into Java `cacerts`
  - disable HTTPS interception in antivirus/proxy if that’s what’s rewriting certificates

Why this matters:
- browsers often trust Windows certificates
- Java often uses its own trust store
- so Chrome/Android Studio can work while standalone Java still fails

If you want, I can do the most useful next step now:
1. add `--input-file` mode to the probe
2. add `--save-html` mode so one fetch can be reused offline
3. then you send me one saved article HTML and I can tune extraction precisely from there

If you want the trust-store route instead, I can guide you step by step too.

```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 2.2 seconds
Output:
import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.URI;
import java.net.http.HttpClient;
import java.net.http.HttpRequest;
import java.net.http.HttpResponse;
import java.security.SecureRandom;
import java.security.cert.X509Certificate;
import java.time.Duration;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.LinkedHashSet;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Locale;
import java.util.Set;
import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
import javax.net.ssl.SSLParameters;
import javax.net.ssl.TrustManager;
import javax.net.ssl.X509TrustManager;
import org.jsoup.Jsoup;
import org.jsoup.nodes.Document;
import org.jsoup.nodes.Element;

public final class WebExtractorProbe {
    private static final List<String> PRIMARY_SELECTORS = List.of(
        "[itemprop=articleBody]",
        "[data-article-body]",
        "[data-module*=article]",
        ".article-body",
        ".article__body",
        ".article-content",
        ".article__content",
        ".story-body",
        ".story-content",
        ".entry-content",
        ".post-content",
        ".node__content",
        ".wysiwyg",
        "article .content",
        "article .body",
        "main article",
        "article"
    );

    private static final List<String> PRUNE_KEYWORDS = List.of(
        "recommend",
        "related",
        "most-read",
        "mostread",
        "most-commented",
        "popular",
        "trending",
        "share",
        "social",
        "newsletter",
        "subscribe",
        "comment",
        "cookie",
        "consent",
        "breadcrumb",
        "promo",
        "advert",
        "ad-container",
        "adslot",
        "taboola",
        "outbrain",
        "next-post",
        "read-more",
        "more-like-this",
        "tag-list",
        "topics",
        "paywall",
        "locked",
        "unlocking",
        "audio-broadcast",
        "call-to-action"
    );

    private static final List<String> SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS = List.of(
        "recommended stories",
        "related reports",
        "read more like this",
        "view comments",
        "unlocking article",
        "help us get the word out",
        "audio broadcast",
        "call to action",
        "please join the",
        "advertisement",
        "ads"
    );

    private static final List<String> TERMINAL_SECTION_HEADINGS = List.of(
        "tags",
        "help us get the word out",
        "audio broadcast",
        "call to action",
        "related reports",
        "next post"
    );

    private static final List<String> STANDALONE_JUNK_HEADINGS = List.of(
        "recommended stories",
        "related reports",
        "read more like this",
        "view comments",
        "audio broadcast",
        "call to action",
        "unlocking article",
        "advertisement"
    );

    private WebExtractorProbe() {
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
        if (args.length == 0) {
            System.err.println("Usage: java WebExtractorProbe [--insecure] <url>");
            System.exit(1);
        }

        boolean insecure = false;
        String url;
        if (args.length >= 2 && "--insecure".equals(args[0])) {
            insecure = true;
            url = args[1].trim();
        } else {
            url = args[0].trim();
        }

        ExtractionResult result = extract(url, insecure);

        System.out.println("URL: " + result.url);
        System.out.println("TITLE: " + result.title);
        System.out.println("LANG: " + result.languageCode);
        System.out.println("TEXT_LENGTH: " + result.extractedText.length());
        System.out.println("LIKELY_INCOMPLETE: " + result.likelyIncomplete);
        System.out.println();
        System.out.println(result.extractedText);
    }

    public static ExtractionResult extract(String url) throws IOException, InterruptedException {
        return extract(url, false);
    }

    public static ExtractionResult extract(String url, boolean insecure) throws IOException, InterruptedException {
        HttpClient.Builder clientBuilder = HttpClient.newBuilder()
            .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL)
            .connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(20));

        if (insecure) {
            clientBuilder.sslContext(buildInsecureSslContext());
            SSLParameters sslParameters = new SSLParameters();
            sslParameters.setEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm("");
            clientBuilder.sslParameters(sslParameters);
        }

        HttpClient client = clientBuilder.build();

        HttpRequest request = HttpRequest.newBuilder(URI.create(url))
            .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(30))
            .header(
                "User-Agent",
                "Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 14) AppleWebKit/537.36 " +
                    "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0 Mobile Safari/537.36"
            )
            .GET()
            .build();

        HttpResponse<String> response = client.send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString());
        if (response.statusCode() < 200 || response.statusCode() >= 300) {
            throw new IOException("Request failed with HTTP " + response.statusCode());
        }

        Document document = Jsoup.parse(response.body(), url);
        sanitize(document);

        Element root = selectBestRoot(document);
        String extractedText = extractReadableText(root != null ? root : document.body());
        String title = resolveTitle(document, url);
        String languageCode = null;
        Element html = document.selectFirst("html");
        if (html != null) {
            String lang = html.attr("lang");
            if (!lang.isBlank()) {
                languageCode = lang.split("-", 2)[0];
            }
        }

        return new ExtractionResult(url, title, languageCode, extractedText, extractedText.length() < 120);
    }

    private static void sanitize(Document document) {
        document.select(
            "script, style, nav, footer, header, noscript, form, iframe, svg, " +
                "button, input, picture, source, video, audio, canvas"
        ).remove();

        List<Element> elements = new ArrayList<>(document.select("*"));
        for (Element element : elements) {
            if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
                element.remove();
            }
        }
    }

    private static Element selectBestRoot(Document document) {
        for (String selector : PRIMARY_SELECTORS) {
            for (Element candidate : document.select(selector)) {
                if (isValidContentRoot(candidate)) {
                    return candidate;
                }
            }
        }

        Element best = null;
        int bestScore = Integer.MIN_VALUE;
        for (Element candidate : document.select("article, main, [role=main], section, div")) {
            int score = scoreContentRoot(candidate);
            if (score > bestScore && isValidContentRoot(candidate)) {
                best = candidate;
                bestScore = score;
            }
        }
        return best;
    }

    private static boolean isValidContentRoot(Element element) {
        if (element == null) {
            return false;
        }
        if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
            return false;
        }
        if (element.text().length() < 180) {
            return false;
        }
        return scoreContentRoot(element) >= 280;
    }

    private static int scoreContentRoot(Element element) {
        int paragraphCount = element.select("p").size();
        int paragraphTextLength = element.select("p").stream().mapToInt(p -> p.text().length()).sum();
        int articleHint = hasArticleHint(element) ? 250 : 0;
        int bodyHint = hasBodyHint(element) ? 180 : 0;
        int junkPenalty = hasJunkHint(element) ? 500 : 0;
        return paragraphTextLength + (paragraphCount * 120) + articleHint + bodyHint - junkPenalty;
    }

    private static String extractReadableText(Element root) {
        if (root == null) {
            return "";
        }

        Element workingRoot = root.clone();
        List<Element> elements = new ArrayList<>(workingRoot.select("*"));
        for (Element element : elements) {
            if (shouldPruneElement(element)) {
                element.remove();
            }
        }

        List<String> blocks = new ArrayList<>();
        Set<String> seen = new LinkedHashSet<>();

        for (Element element : workingRoot.select("h2, h3, h4, p, li, blockquote, pre")) {
            if (shouldSkipTextElement(element)) {
                continue;
            }

            String text = normalizeText(element.text());
            if (text.isBlank()) {
                continue;
            }

            if (isTerminalSectionHeading(text, element)) {
                break;
            }

            if (isSkippableStandaloneText(text, element)) {
                continue;
            }

            int minimumLength = element.tagName().startsWith("h") ? 12 : 28;
            if (text.length() < minimumLength) {
                continue;
            }

            if (seen.add(text)) {
                blocks.add(text);
            }
        }

        if (!blocks.isEmpty()) {
            return String.join("\n\n", blocks);
        }

        String[] fragments = normalizeText(workingRoot.text())
            .split("(?<=[.!?\\u3002\\uFF01\\uFF1F])\\s+");
        List<String> fallback = new ArrayList<>();
        Set<String> fallbackSeen = new LinkedHashSet<>();
        for (String fragment : fragments) {
            String cleaned = normalizeText(fragment);
            if (cleaned.length() >= 30 && fallbackSeen.add(cleaned)) {
                fallback.add(cleaned);
            }
        }
        return String.join("\n\n", fallback);
    }

    private static String resolveTitle(Document document, String url) {
        Element metaTitle = document.selectFirst("meta[property=og:title]");
        if (metaTitle != null) {
            String content = metaTitle.attr("content").trim();
            if (!content.isBlank()) {
                return content;
            }
        }

        String title = document.title().trim();
        return title.isBlank() ? url : title;
    }

    private static boolean shouldPruneElement(Element element) {
        String ownText = normalizeText(element.ownText());
        String combinedHints = (
            element.tagName() + " " +
                element.id() + " " +
                element.className() + " " +
                element.attr("role") + " " +
                element.attr("aria-label") + " " +
                element.attr("data-testid") + " " +
                element.attr("data-module")
            ).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);

        if (combinedHints.contains("article-body") || combinedHints.contains("article__body")) {
            return false;
        }

        if (containsAny(combinedHints, PRUNE_KEYWORDS)) {
            return true;
        }

        if (!Set.of("article", "main", "body").contains(element.tagName())) {
            for (Element heading : element.select("h1, h2, h3, h4, h5")) {
                if (isStandaloneJunkHeading(normalizeText(heading.text()))) {
                    return true;
                }
            }
        }

        if (hasAdLikeHint(combinedHints) && !hasArticleHint(element)) {
            return true;
        }

        return isStandaloneJunkHeading(ownText);
    }

    private static boolean shouldSkipTextElement(Element element) {
        String text = normalizeText(element.text());
        if (text.isBlank()) {
            return true;
        }
        for (Element parent : element.parents()) {
            if (shouldPruneElement(parent)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return containsAny(text.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT), SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS);
    }

    private static boolean isSkippableStandaloneText(String text, Element element) {
        if (containsAny(text.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT), SKIP_TEXT_PATTERNS)) {
            return true;
        }
        return element.tagName().equals("li") && text.length() < 45;
    }

    private static boolean isTerminalSectionHeading(String text, Element element) {
        if (!element.tagName().startsWith("h")) {
            return false;
        }
        String normalized = text.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
        for (String marker : TERMINAL_SECTION_HEADINGS) {
            if (normalized.equals(marker) || normalized.startsWith(marker + ":")) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private static boolean isStandaloneJunkHeading(String text) {
        if (text.isBlank()) {
            return false;
        }
        String normalized = text.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
        for (String marker : STANDALONE_JUNK_HEADINGS) {
            if (normalized.equals(marker) || normalized.startsWith(marker + ":")) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private static boolean hasArticleHint(Element element) {
        String hints = (
            element.id() + " " +
                element.className() + " " +
                element.attr("itemprop")
            ).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
        return containsAny(hints, List.of("article", "story", "entry", "post", "content"));
    }

    private static boolean hasBodyHint(Element element) {
        String hints = (
            element.id() + " " +
                element.className() + " " +
                element.attr("itemprop")
            ).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
        return containsAny(hints, List.of("body", "main", "text", "copy", "content"));
    }

    private static boolean hasJunkHint(Element element) {
        String hints = (element.id() + " " + element.className()).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);
        return containsAny(hints, PRUNE_KEYWORDS);
    }

    private static boolean hasAdLikeHint(String text) {
        return text.matches(".*(^|[^a-z])(ad|ads|advert|adslot)([^a-z]|$).*");
    }

    private static boolean containsAny(String text, List<String> values) {
        for (String value : values) {
            if (text.contains(value.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT))) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private static String normalizeText(String text) {
        String normalized = text.replace('\u00A0', ' ');
        normalized = normalized.replaceAll("[\\t\\x0B\\f\\r ]+", " ");
        normalized = normalized.replaceAll("\\n{3,}", "\n\n");

        String[] lines = normalized.split("\\R");
        List<String> cleaned = new ArrayList<>();
        for (String line : lines) {
            String trimmed = line.trim();
            if (!trimmed.isBlank()) {
                cleaned.add(trimmed);
            }
        }
        return String.join("\n", cleaned).trim();
    }

    public record ExtractionResult(
        String url,
        String title,
        String languageCode,
        String extractedText,
        boolean likelyIncomplete
    ) {}

    private static SSLContext buildInsecureSslContext() {
        try {
            TrustManager[] trustAll = new TrustManager[] {
                new X509TrustManager() {
                    @Override
                    public void checkClientTrusted(X509Certificate[] chain, String authType) {
                    }

                    @Override
                    public void checkServerTrusted(X509Certificate[] chain, String authType) {
                    }

                    @Override
                    public X509Certificate[] getAcceptedIssuers() {
                        return new X509Certificate[0];
                    }
                }
            };

            SSLContext sslContext = SSLContext.getInstance("TLS");
            sslContext.init(null, trustAll, new SecureRandom());
            return sslContext;
        } catch (Exception error) {
            throw new IllegalStateException("Couldn't build insecure SSL context", error);
        }
    }
}
```

```
}
```
